headline: E-Waste Recycling Covers Only 15% of Annual Generation
context: DOE's 2025 report found Malaysia generates 365,000 tonnes of e-waste annually but only 54,750 tonnes are processed through licensed recyclers.

cards.sub: Their combined annual capacity is 120,000 tonnes. Actual collection reaches only 54,750 — the rest enters informal channels.

cards.big: Informal e-waste processing in Klang Valley employs 8,000 workers.
cards.sub: Acid baths and open burning to extract metals release lead and mercury into residential areas.

cards.big: Children in Kepong informal recycling areas show 3x normal blood lead levels.
cards.sub: A 2024 UM medical study tested 200 children within 1km of unlicensed operations.

cards.big: Japan recovers 98% of rare metals from e-waste. Malaysia recovers 12%.
cards.sub: Urban mining of e-waste is worth an estimated RM2.4B annually in recoverable materials.

cards.big: E-waste is not garbage. It is a RM2.4B resource being processed by children with acid.
Formalising e-waste recovery requires making licensed recycling cheaper than informal alternatives. Subsidy and enforcement must work together.
